channel.title: Latest alerts from washingtonpost.com
channel.description: An unofficial feed created by Derek Willis.

item.title: Supreme Court backs Alabama’s bid to redraw congressional districts, setting stage for state to enact map that benefits GOP
item.description: In an unsigned opinion, the justices vacated a lower court’s finding that a 2023 redistricting map had diluted the political power of the state’s Black voters. Monday’s ruling could deliver an additional seat to the GOP in the midterm elections. The order sent the case back to the lower court for consideration under the Supreme Court’s ideologically split April decision on the Voting Rights Act.

item.title: Should you be worried about hantavirus? Watch ‘Make It Make Sense.’
item.description: Should you be worried about hantavirus? Watch ‘Make It Make Sense.’

item.title: Virginia Democrats make emergency appeal to U.S. Supreme Court to save new House maps that would give party a boost
item.description: Virginia Democrats argue that the state’s high court improperly overturned a redistricting referendum last week. But some top Democrats express little hope that the appeal will affect this November’s congressional midterms and are pivoting to waging campaigns in the state’s existing districts.

item.title: The parents who see themselves in the Reiner family tragedy
item.description: Rob and Michele Reiner were found stabbed to death in their Los Angeles home on Dec. 14. Hours later, their younger son was arrested and charged in their killings. Families who have experienced the same cycle of drug abuse and mental illness are following the murder case with dread: Could this happen to us?

item.title: Where record-breaking summerlike heat will hit the U.S. this week
item.description: Record heat is expected to sizzle across 22 states this week — with the most intense conditions expected across the Intermountain West, Plains and South. Temperatures are forecast to top 90 degrees for about 50 million people and 100 degrees for 11 million more.

item.title: Trump is stuck in an Iran trap of his making — with only two options
item.description: The president launched the attacks without preparation. Now he has to finish the job.

item.title: Trump seeks to pause federal gas tax as prices soar amid Iran conflict
item.description: Congress would have to approve the proposal, and passing the savings on to drivers, rather than oil companies, would be difficult to enforce. The plan reflects President Donald Trump’s limited options to address surging gas prices as negotiations to end the war remain stuck with no immediate way to reopen key shipping lanes.

item.title: In Trump administration battle over AI, U.S. spy agencies seek more power
item.description: As the White House grapples with cybersecurity threats from artificial intelligence models, intelligence officials want sway in AI policy overseen by Commerce.

item.title: How passengers from the hantavirus-hit cruise ship are quarantining in the United States
item.description: More than a dozen Americans who were on the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak are being watched at the Nebraska-based National Quarantine Unit.

item.title: Nonprofit sues to stop Trump’s changes to Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
item.description: The Cultural Landscape Foundation, an education and advocacy organization, asked a federal court to halt President Donald Trump’s efforts to resurface the Reflecting Pool, saying that the president failed to undergo required federal reviews before applying a blue color to the basin.

item.title: Second weddings should be low-key and private, right? Carolyn Hax responds.
item.description: Second weddings should be low-key and private, right? Carolyn Hax responds.

item.title: Roughly 1 in 4 Americans think the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner was faked, a poll finds
item.description: Roughly 1 in 3 Democrats said they believed the incident was staged, compared with about 1 in 8 Republicans, according to a survey published by NewsGuard. The poll also found that many Americans believe two attempts to assassinate Donald Trump in 2024 were faked.

item.title: How ChatGPT’s fixation with goblins ended and what to know about secret commands
item.description: Experiment with the system prompts that steer the behavior of chatbots, and see how they change the way AI rewrites this article.

item.title: The hilarious Marco Rubio meme is making America laugh together
item.description: The jokes provide rare bipartisan smiles during vicious culture war.

item.title: These files are in today’s On the Record
item.description: These files are in today’s On the Record

item.title: Iran talks. Xi Jinping. Trump conspiracy theories. Hantavirus. Record heat. AI’s hidden rules. Plastic chemicals. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: Republicans who denied Biden’s 2020 win could be governors next year. Winners will have oversight of 2028 elections in key states.
item.description: Political figures who took leading roles in trying to reverse Donald Trump’s presidential loss appear poised to win GOP nominations for governor in swing states including Arizona, Pennsylvania and Wisconsin.

item.title: The Colorado River is nearing a breaking point (again)
item.description: The snowpacks that feed the river are the smallest on record, and the reservoirs that hold the majority of its water are nearing historic lows. A stopgap proposal from Arizona, California and Nevada is unlikely to break the stalemate in negotiations over the future of the river.

item.title: Fulfilling a lifelong dream, a 72-year-old will graduate from medical school
item.description: Dawn Zuidgeest-Craft, who worked as a nurse practitioner for four decades while raising four kids, will start a three-year residency at a Michigan hospital in July. She hopes to work as a doctor as long as she can.

item.title: Still need to finish this weekend’s On the Record? We’ve got you.
item.description: Play now to finish out your score for the week.

item.title: Passengers on hantavirus-hit cruise ship begin returning to U.S. and other home countries for monitoring
item.description: Seventeen Americans are expected to head to a federal quarantine facility in Nebraska, where public health officials say they will be “assessing and monitoring” each individual.

item.title: I wanted answers about my mother’s early life. Then I found a dusty scrapbook.
item.description: "The scrapbook’s clippings paint a picture of a simple, gentle, community-based life," John Ficarra writes.

item.title: I wanted to hate this weird accessory. Then I slept soundly in economy, our travel reporter writes.
item.description: Can a glorified tube top help you sleep during air travel? Our reporter tested it on many long flights.

item.title: Trump’s ‘golden visa’ for global elite is risky, legally dubious, lawyers say
item.description: Even the first lady’s former attorney is warning people away from the president’s program, which was designed to make immigration easier for the wealthy.

item.title: Trump official says she’s involved in policy changes that benefit her family’s ranches, video shows
item.description: A top Trump appointee at the Interior Department acknowledged that she has been involved in changes to grazing policies that benefit ranching businesses like her family’s, according to a video of her remarks — a claim that some ethics experts say could violate federal law.

item.title: My ambassador landlord wanted his house back. I wanted to find my way home.
item.description: What is home? When my D.C. dwelling was in jeopardy and my employment future was murky, my search began.

item.title: Bobby Cox, Hall of Fame manager of the Atlanta Braves, dies at 84
item.description: He built a baseball dynasty in Atlanta while winning more than 2,500 games during a 29-year managerial career.

item.title: Forced to dance for Mengele at Auschwitz, she was called to help others heal
item.description: Psychologist Edith Eger, who has died at 98, treated veterans and abuse victims. She had to heal herself first, she said, to truly help her patients.

item.title: Inside Ben Shapiro’s MAGA meltdown
item.description: The Daily Wire was once ascendant in right-wing media. Now, the “anti-woke” company faces contentious layoffs, ideological battles and dwindling relevance online.

item.title: An American industrial revolution is brewing. David Ignatius saw it in Pittsburgh.
item.description: America isn’t ready for “Day 30.” Start-ups like Gecko Robotics are working to change that.

item.title: Want to track the apocalypse? One theory: Follow the billionaires’ jets.
item.description: A Los Angeles artist and coder has created an app that tracks private and charter flights, based on his theory that the ultra-rich will flee city centers before a nuclear apocalypse.

item.title: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.
item.description: How many guesses will Keyword take you today? Try to beat the average now.

item.title: DOJ sees fallout after push to prosecute former FBI director James Comey
item.description: Several prosecutors have left the Justice Department, others are considering doing so, and at least one major case has been disrupted.

item.title: D.C.’s public golf courses will stay open under a new deal with the Trump administration
item.description: The nonprofit National Links Trust will continue to run the three courses. But the future of East Potomac Golf Links, eyed by President Donald Trump for an overhaul, remains unclear.

item.title: Column: Tips for drinking good wine without breaking the bank
item.description: It doesn’t matter if your budget is modest or magnum-sized, or if you just want to sip something delicious and affordable when the mood strikes. Here’s how to get the most out of every dollar you spend.

item.title: Her true role in Elon Musk’s life was a secret. Now she’s central to his courtroom fight.
item.description: In hours of testimony, Musk’s partner Shivon Zilis opened up about their relationship and her role in creating one of the world’s most valuable artificial intelligence companies.

item.title: Why young and old men are leaving the labor force at record rates
item.description: The share of American men working or searching for a job recently hit the lowest level since 1948, aside from the pandemic, Labor Department data shows.

item.title: White House prepares to replace FDA Commissioner Marty Makary amid agency turmoil
item.description: Makary has faced criticism over his leadership of the agency that oversees vaccine and drug approvals.

item.title: Inside the probe that has 13 top D.C. police officials fighting for their jobs
item.description: The 554-page internal affairs report, investigators say, paints a picture of D.C. police officials manipulating classifications as they buckled under pressure to reduce crime and avoid the ire of higher-ups.

item.title: Carolyn Hax chat: Should I tell an ex why I really broke up with him?
item.description: Our advice columnist answers your questions about the strange train we call life.

item.title: Melania Trump: Raising America’s next generation
item.description: Women can lead boldly at work while restoring the honor of motherhood.

item.title: Anti-woke Vivek Ramaswamy confronts racism in his run for Ohio governor
item.description: Vivek Ramaswamy built a political brand casting racism as an obsession of Democrats. As a Republican running for Ohio governor, he’s facing racism on the right.

item.title: In win for GOP, Virginia Supreme Court strikes down redistricting referendum
item.description: The finding is a major setback to Democrats in their effort to counter GOP-led redistricting in other states. The measure, approved in April, gave Democrats an edge in four districts.

item.title: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.
item.description: Wager your points and finish off the week with today’s On the Record.

item.title: U.S. economy adds 115,000 jobs in April, a strong gain for an uncertain labor market
item.description: The unemployment rate held at 4.3 percent, according to a Labor Department report. But the effects of the Iran war are only beginning to emerge.

item.title: Iran strikes. Trump’s tariffs. Election maps. Disaster funds. Elon Musk. Working men. AI clones. Catch up now.
item.description: Catch up in minutes with these 7 stories.

item.title: U.S. launches strikes on Iran in response to attacks on American warships, military says
item.description: The “self-defense strikes” followed attacks on three American naval vessels, though none of the warships were hit, U.S. Central Command said in a statement.

item.title: Correction: Trump-appointed panel calls for overhauling how FEMA operates. An earlier alert incorrectly said the report included plans to cut staffing in half.
item.description:
In its final report, the FEMA Review Council also recommended shifting leadership of emergency response and recovery to the states.
An earlier version of this alert incorrectly said the report included plans to cut staffing in half. This alert has been corrected.

item.title: Trade court delivers new blow to President Donald Trump in his effort to impose widespread tariffs
item.description: The Court of International Trade ruled against the import tax the president imposed after losing on tariffs at the Supreme Court.

item.title: Sen. John Fetterman: I haven’t changed. Here’s what has.
item.description: John Fetterman: I haven’t changed. Here’s what has.
